title: identity — compliance evidence
description: Identity is the most compliance-sensitive aggregate in the platform. Every enforceable rule from GDPR (Art. 5, 6, 17, 25, 32), COPPA (13-year-old floor), CCPA (right-to-know + right-to-delete), and NIST 800-63B (authentication policy) applies here. Every enforcement is codified as CODE + AUDIT rather than a policy document; the compliance officer verifies via automated checks against the entries below.

controls.evidence: CompliantAgePolicy resolves the effective floor from `compliance.coppa.age_threshold` (13 for US country_code) BEFORE creation. Identity creation refuses with `IDENTITY_MINOR_UNDER_FLOOR` (422) when dob resolves the applicant to under-13. Parent-consent flows for tenants that legitimately serve under-13s live in the sports/athletes module; the raw Identity substrate refuses the write.

For identities resolved as minors, `dob` classification is promoted from `restricted` to `regulated_minor` (see data-classes.json). Additional redaction rules apply. Audit trails carry an `is_minor=true` marker so DSR responses correctly scope the child's data package.
Identity carries email + password + MFA + dob ONLY. No name, phone, address — those live on `user::Profile` per Application. No cross-app metadata on the Identity row.
Soft-deleted identities are hard-deleted after `identity.retention.erasure_hold_days` (default 30 days). See retention.json. PurgeSoftDeletedIdentitiesJob is the automated enforcer.
Credentials are necessary to perform the contract with the tenant. Legal basis stored in the initial IdentityRegistered audit-log entry.
For EU identities, CompliantAgePolicy reads `compliance.gdpr.per_country[country_code].minor_age` (13–16 per country). Same refusal path as COPPA when dob resolves the applicant below the floor.

`identity:erase` Artisan command + POST /api/v1/platform/identities/{id} DELETE endpoint both trigger soft delete + start the hold-period. Hard delete cascades to `user::User`, `user::PlatformUser`, `mfa::MfaChallenge`, `auth::PasswordResetToken`, `auth::EmailVerificationToken`. IdentityErased event notifies every listener.

controls.principle: data protection by design and by default
controls.evidence: Password_hash uses argon2id. Mfa_secret is envelope-encrypted (KMS or Vault). Email is CITEXT (removing case-based dedup bugs that could leak related identities). Password_history_hashed retains only bcrypt/argon2id output, never plaintext.

MFA-secret encryption at rest, lockout with progressive backoff, breach-check via haveibeenpwned, mandatory audit logging via HasActivityLog. All enforced by CODE + tested by automated compliance probes.

SuspiciousLoginNotification fires on high-velocity + novel-IP lockouts. IdentityLocked events > threshold in a rolling window trigger the platform-security incident-response pipeline (out-of-scope for this module; handled by observability).

The Data Subject Access Request (DSAR) flow reads the Identity row (unredacted PII) into the exported package. IdentityRepository::exportForDsar() is the entrypoint; audit-logged with the compliance officer as actor.
Same as GDPR Art. 17 path. CCPA's 45-day fulfillment window is enforced by the erasure_hold_days config (defaulted to 30 days for safety).
Not applicable — Identity data is never sold, shared, or transferred to third-party analytics. See subprocessors.json.

Password (memorized secret) + TOTP or WebAuthn (single-factor OTP or single-factor cryptographic device) satisfies AAL2. Config defaults require MFA for admin-role identities via `identity/mfa`; roles below admin can opt in.

Password min length 12 (spec floor 8, we exceed). Breach check against public corpus via HIBP. No composition rules that force predictable patterns (require_symbol is default OFF per NIST recommendation). No mandatory rotation by age (max_age_days default null per NIST recommendation — rotation only on suspected compromise).
identity.rate_limit_login middleware enforces 5 attempts / 15 minutes per (IP, email). identity.check_lockout enforces the account-level lockout.
Two guards enforced (sanctum, platform_admin) with role scoping. All admin operations on Identity are audit-logged. Password hashes use argon2id.
Rate limiting + progressive lockout + suspicious-login detection. All threats emit metrics + alerts.
Health probes (identity-db-writable, identity-kms-reachable, identity-hibp-reachable, identity-lockout-scheduler-alive, identity-retention-current) + OpenTelemetry metrics feed monitoring pipeline.
